name: GPT-Generated Essays Increased Score-Level and Subgroup Training Samples
statement: Zhang, Badola, Johnson, and Li generated GPT-4 and GPT-4o essays by prompt and score level to augment AI essay scorer training samples, then evaluated score agreement and subgroup fairness on held-out student essays.
population_or_context: Seven essay prompts with human-scored student essays on a six-point scale.
quantitative_note: The paper reports that score levels 1, 5, and 6 had few student essays, and that GPT augmentation substantially increased training samples at each score level.
evaluation_method: QWK, correlation, SMD, subgroup MDSS, subgroup QWK, and feature comparisons between student and generated essays.
sparsity_or_imbalance_motivation: Small samples for some score levels and subgroups could cause scorer performance and fairness issues.
synthetic_data_research_role: Generated essays were used for scorer training augmentation, performance benchmarking, and subgroup fairness analysis.
reported_metrics: Human-AI QWK, correlations, SMD, MDSS, subgroup QWK; augmented models generally performed comparably to student-only models and mitigated some subgroup differences.
limitations: Only one human rating was available.
Generated essays may differ from student essays in subtle content and style ways not fully captured by surface features.
source_locator: Abstract; Sections 3.2-3.3; Base and augmented scoring model training; Results for RQ2 and RQ3; Limitations.
